welcome.title: Writing Studio
welcome.body: A dockable workspace for this book. Panels (compose, planner, cast, quality…) will be added here one at a time. Drag tabs to split, stack, float, or pop out into their own window.
welcome.paletteHint: ⌘⇧P → "Choose Your Focus" or "Start Guided Tour" anytime

compose.title: Compose
compose.guideBody: The AI co-writer for this book — chat with it about a scene, ask it to draft or revise a passage, or brainstorm what happens next. Open it when you want a conversational partner that already knows this book's characters, glossary, and prior chapters.

editor.title: Editor
editor.guideBody: The main manuscript editor where you write and edit chapter text directly. Open it whenever you're drafting new prose or making direct line edits — it's the canvas everything else (Compose, Planner, Steering) feeds into.

planner.title: Planner
planner.guideBody: Lay out the novel's structure — arcs, chapters, beats, and how they connect — before or while you write. Open it when you need to see or rework the shape of the story, not just the sentence in front of you.

usage.title: Usage
usage.guideBody: Shows how much you're spending across every AI-powered action — translations, enrichment, chat, compose — broken down by tokens and requests over time. Open it when you want to check your current spend against your budget, spot which feature is burning the most credits, or review a specific request's cost after the fact.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.guideBody: Collects job completions, translation runs, enrichment passes, and system alerts in one place so you don't have to keep checking back on long-running work. Open it after kicking off a big job (like translating a chapter or running enrichment) to know the moment it finishes, or to catch anything that failed and needs attention.

settings.title: Settings
settings.guideBody: Manage your account, connect your own AI providers (OpenAI, local models, etc.), and configure translation defaults like target language and glossary behavior. Open it before starting a new project to set things up, or any time you need to swap providers, update credentials, or change how translations behave by default.

trash.title: Trash
trash.guideBody: Holds books, chapters, and other items you've deleted, keeping them recoverable instead of gone for good. Open it when you've deleted something by mistake and need to restore it, or when you want to permanently clear out old items you no longer need.

steering.title: Steering
steering.desc: Author rules that guide AI on this book
steering.guideBody: Write the rules and preferences that guide how the AI writes for this specific book — tone, POV, things to avoid, recurring instructions you don't want to repeat every chat. Open it once early on, then revisit whenever the AI's output drifts from what you want.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.desc: Entities, search, and bulk actions for this book
glossary.guideBody: Your book's living dictionary of characters, places, items, and other named entities — search, filter, and bulk-edit them from one place. Open it when you need to check what's already been established, fix a name across many entries at once, or clean up entities in bulk.

glossary-ontology.title: Ontology
glossary-ontology.desc: Kinds, genres & attributes for this book
glossary-ontology.guideBody: Defines the kinds (character, location, item, etc.), genres, and custom attributes available for classifying entities in this book. Open it before you start tagging entities, or whenever you need to add a new kind or attribute that doesn't fit the existing set.

glossary-unknown.title: Unknown entities
glossary-unknown.desc: Reassign unrecognized entities to a kind
glossary-unknown.guideBody: Lists entities the system spotted in your text but couldn't confidently classify into a kind. Open it periodically to assign these unrecognized names to the right kind so they show up correctly in the Glossary and Wiki instead of sitting in limbo.

glossary-ai-suggestions.title: AI suggestions
glossary-ai-suggestions.guideBody: Shows entity entries the AI drafted automatically from your manuscript, waiting for your review. Open it to accept, edit, or reject each suggestion before it becomes part of your official glossary.

glossary-merge-candidates.title: Merge candidates
glossary-merge-candidates.guideBody: Flags pairs of entities the system thinks might be duplicates (e.g. two spellings of the same character's name). Open it to confirm real duplicates and merge them, or dismiss false matches, keeping your glossary clean.

wiki.title: Wiki
wiki.desc: Browse and read this book's generated wiki articles
wiki.guideBody: The auto-generated wiki of readable articles built from your glossary and story content — one article per major entity. Open it when you want a narrative-style overview of a character, place, or faction instead of raw glossary fields.

wiki-editor.title: Wiki Editor
wiki-editor.desc: Edit a wiki article's prose, infobox, history and suggestions

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.desc: Browse and open your knowledge-graph projects
knowledge.guideBody: Knowledge is your entry point into a book's knowledge graph — the entities, events, and lore your AI agents have extracted and organized. Open it to pick which project (book) to work in before diving into entities, timelines, or the graph itself.

kg-overview.title: Overview
kg-overview.guideBody: Overview gives you a snapshot of a project's health at a glance — entity counts, recent activity, and pending work. Open it first when you return to a book to see what needs attention before drilling into a specific tool.

kg-entities.title: Entities
kg-entities.desc: Browse and search knowledge-graph entities, scoped to a project or across all your projects
kg-entities.guideBody: Entities lists every character, place, item, and concept the system has extracted, with search and filters to narrow by kind or project. Use it to look up a specific entity's details or to audit what the knowledge graph currently knows about your world.

kg-timeline.title: Timeline
kg-timeline.desc: Browse in-story events by chronological or narrative order
kg-timeline.guideBody: Timeline lays out the events of your story in order — either the sequence they happened in-world (chronological) or the sequence they were revealed to the reader (narrative). Open it to check consistency in your plot's sequencing or to see what's happened so far at any point in the story.

kg-evidence.title: Evidence
kg-evidence.desc: Search chapter and chat passages by semantic similarity
kg-evidence.guideBody: Evidence lets you search your chapters and chat history by meaning rather than exact wording, surfacing passages that discuss a topic even if they don't use the same phrasing. Use it when you need to find where something was mentioned or established, but can't remember the exact words.

kg-gap.title: Gap Report
kg-gap.desc: Find high-mention characters, places, and concepts missing from the glossary
kg-gap.guideBody: Gap Report surfaces characters, places, and concepts that come up often in your text but haven't been added to the glossary yet. Check it periodically to catch lore that's slipping through without a proper entry.

kg-proposals.title: Proposals
kg-proposals.desc: Review pending glossary, wiki, and lore-enrichment suggestions for this book
kg-proposals.guideBody: Proposals is where AI-suggested additions and changes to your glossary, wiki, and lore wait for your review. Open it to approve, edit, or reject each suggestion before it becomes part of your book's canon.

kg-schema.title: Schema
kg-schema.desc: Adopt, author, view, and sync your book's knowledge-graph schema
kg-schema.guideBody: Schema controls the structure of your book's knowledge graph — what kinds of entities and relationships it recognizes. Use it to adopt a starting schema, customize it for your world, or sync changes across your project.

kg-graph.title: Knowledge Graph
kg-graph.desc: Explore the project's entity relationship graph
kg-graph.guideBody: Knowledge Graph gives you a visual map of how entities in your project connect — who knows whom, what belongs where, which events involve which characters. Open it when you want to explore relationships spatially rather than as a list.

kg-insights.title: Mining Insights
kg-insights.desc: Extraction config quality, model performance & outcome drift across projects
kg-insights.guideBody: Mining Insights reports on how well extraction is performing across your projects — config quality, which models are producing good results, and where outcomes are drifting from expectations. Check it if extracted entities or events seem off, to diagnose whether the issue is the model or the config.

kg-jobs.title: Extraction Jobs
kg-jobs.desc: Monitor knowledge-extraction jobs across all your projects
kg-jobs.guideBody: Extraction Jobs shows the status of every knowledge-extraction run across all your projects, so you can see what's queued, in progress, done, or failed. Open it to check on long-running extractions or troubleshoot one that didn't finish as expected.

kg-bio.title: Global Bio
kg-bio.desc: Your cross-book author bio, used to ground AI writing and analysis
kg-bio.guideBody: Global Bio holds a short author bio that's shared across all your books, used to give AI writing and analysis tools context about your voice and background. Fill it in once and every project benefits from the same grounding.

kg-privacy.title: Privacy & Data
kg-privacy.desc: Export or permanently delete your knowledge-graph data
kg-privacy.guideBody: Privacy & Data lets you export a full copy of your knowledge-graph data or permanently delete it. Use it when you want a backup, are leaving the platform, or need to remove a project's data for good.

jobs-list.title: Jobs
jobs-list.guideBody: Shows the background jobs running for this book — translation runs, enrichment passes, exports, and other long-running tasks — along with their current status and progress. Open it to check whether a job is still running, see if one failed, or confirm a task finished before you move on.

job-detail.title: Job
job-detail.empty: Open a job from the Jobs panel to watch it here.

books.title: Books
books.desc: Browse and read your other books without leaving this studio
books.guideBody: Opens a lightweight library view of your other books right inside the studio, so you can check details, reread a passage, or jump between projects without losing your current editing session. Use it when you want quick cross-book reference without navigating away from what you're writing.

book-reader.title: Reader
book-reader.desc: Distraction-free reading view for a chapter

leaderboard-books.title: Leaderboard: Books
leaderboard-books.desc: Top-ranked books by rating, readers & favorites
leaderboard-books.guideBody: Shows which books are climbing the rankings by rating, reader count, and favorites, so you can see what's resonating with readers right now. Check this panel when you want a pulse on what's working across the platform, or to scout comparable titles in your genre.

leaderboard-authors.title: Leaderboard: Authors
leaderboard-authors.guideBody: Ranks authors by how many readers they've reached and how much they've published, giving you a sense of who's active and who's building an audience. Open it if you're curious how your output and reach compare, or looking for prolific authors to follow.

leaderboard-translators.title: Leaderboard: Translators
leaderboard-translators.guideBody: Ranks translators by chapters completed and number of languages covered, highlighting who's driving the platform's translation activity. Open this when you want to find active translators to collaborate with, or gauge how much translation work is happening across languages.

leaderboard-trending.title: Leaderboard: Trending
leaderboard-trending.guideBody: Surfaces the books gaining momentum right now, based on recent reader activity rather than all-time totals. Check it when you want to catch rising titles early or see what's currently capturing attention.

context-inspector.title: Context Inspector
context-inspector.desc: Trace what context management did per turn — budget, allocation, Planner→Compiler decisions
context-inspector.guideBody: Shows exactly what context was sent to the AI for a given turn — the token budget, what got included or dropped, and whether a quick pass or a fuller planning pass handled it. Open it when the AI's response seems off and you want to see what it actually "saw", or when you're curious how your context budget is being spent.

chapter-browser.title: Chapter Browser
chapter-browser.desc: Sort, filter, search and bulk-act across this book's chapters
chapter-browser.bulk_not_wired: Coming soon — this bulk action isn't wired up yet
chapter-browser.client_filtered_note: Word-count/date filters apply to the currently loaded page
chapter-browser.guideBody: A sortable, filterable list of every chapter in the book, with search and bulk actions (like retagging or reordering several chapters at once). Open it when you need to find a specific chapter fast or make the same change across many chapters at once.

sharing.title: Sharing
sharing.desc: Visibility, unlisted link, and collaborators for this book
sharing.guideBody: Control who can see and edit this book — set it to private, generate an unlisted share link for anyone with the URL, or invite specific collaborators. Open this when you want to share a draft for feedback or hand off editing access without making the book public.

book-settings.title: Book Settings
book-settings.desc: Title, cover, genre tags, and world link for this book
book-settings.guideBody: Manage this book's core identity: its title, cover image, genre tags, and the world it's linked to. Open this when you're setting up a new book or need to update how it's labeled and categorized before sharing or publishing.

translation.title: Translation
translation.desc: Coverage matrix, language filter, bulk translate and glossary extraction for this book
translation.guideBody: Shows every language's translation progress for this book at a glance, with a coverage matrix and language filters so you can spot what's untranslated or stale. Open it to kick off bulk translation jobs or extract glossary terms discovered during translation, keeping terminology consistent across languages.

translation-versions.title: Translation Versions
translation-versions.desc: Per-chapter translation version management (compare, set active, re-translate)
translation-versions.empty: Open a chapter's translation versions from the Translation matrix.

translation-review.title: Translation Review
translation-review.desc: Block-aligned side-by-side review with per-block correction
translation-review.empty: Open a version's Review from the Translation Versions panel.

enrichment-compose.title: Create Enrichment
enrichment-compose.desc: Compose a new enriched lore draft (expand a draft, paste context, upload files, or state an intent)
enrichment-compose.guideBody: Use this panel to start a new lore draft when the wiki or glossary is missing an entry you need — expand a rough draft, paste in reference context, upload source files, or just describe your intent and let the AI generate a first pass. It's the entry point for turning "I need a bio for this character" into a structured, editable proposal.

enrichment-proposals.title: Enrichment Proposals
enrichment-proposals.desc: Review AI-proposed lore — approve, reject, or promote into canon
enrichment-proposals.guideBody: Every draft created in Compose (or generated by an enrichment job) lands here for review before it becomes canon — read through each proposed entry, approve it to promote it into the glossary, or reject it if it's wrong or unwanted. Open this panel whenever you want to check what the AI has drafted but hasn't yet been added to your book's official lore.

enrichment-gaps.title: Enrichment Gaps
enrichment-gaps.desc: Detect under-described entities and auto-enrich them as a background job
enrichment-gaps.guideBody: This panel scans your book for entities that are mentioned but under-described — characters, places, or items with too little detail in the glossary — and can queue them for automatic enrichment as a background job. Open it when you want to find and fill lore holes without manually hunting for what's missing.

enrichment-sources.title: Enrichment Sources
enrichment-sources.desc: License-tagged source corpora for retrieval and recook grounding
enrichment-sources.guideBody: Manage the source corpora (reference texts, uploaded documents, licensed material) that enrichment and recook jobs pull from for grounding, each tagged with its license so you know what's safe to reuse. Check here when you want to see what source material is feeding your AI-generated drafts, or to add new grounding material.

enrichment-jobs.title: Enrichment Jobs
enrichment-jobs.desc: Monitor and resume cost-capped enrichment jobs
enrichment-jobs.guideBody: Track the status of enrichment jobs you've kicked off — see what's running, completed, or paused, watch cost accumulate against the job's cap, and resume any job that stopped early. Open this panel when you want to check progress or pick back up a cost-capped enrichment run.

enrichment-settings.title: Enrichment Profile
enrichment-settings.desc: Author this book's de-bias profile (worldview, language, era, voice)
enrichment-settings.guideBody: Author this book's de-bias profile here — set the intended worldview, language conventions, era, and narrative voice so enrichment and compose drafts come out consistent with your book's tone instead of a generic default. Set this up early, before running enrichment jobs, so generated lore matches your book from the start.

json-editor.title: JSON
json-editor.desc: Raw JSON view of a resource

media-version-history.title: Version History
media-version-history.desc: Compare and restore past versions of an image or video block

original-source.title: Original Source
original-source.desc: The untranslated source text for this chapter
original-source.empty: Open a chapter from the Editor to view its original source.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.desc: Manage plugins, skills, MCP servers, commands & hooks
extensions.guideBody: Install and manage everything that adds capability to your agent — plugins, skills, MCP server connections, custom commands, and hooks. Open it when you want to add a new tool the agent can use, review what's currently enabled, or disable something that's misbehaving.

proposals.title: Proposals
proposals.guideBody: Lists skills the agent has suggested creating on its own, based on patterns it noticed in your work, so you can review and approve them before they become part of your toolkit. Open it after a session where the agent mentioned it could automate something, to decide whether to accept, edit, or reject the suggestion.

user-guide.title: User Guide
user-guide.desc: Every Studio tool, grouped by area — generated from the same catalog as the Command Palette
user-guide.guideBody: Lists every tool and panel in Writing Studio, grouped by area, with a plain-language explanation of what each one does. It's generated straight from the same catalog that powers the Command Palette and dock, so as new tools are added the guide updates automatically — it never falls out of sync with what's actually in the app.

agent-mode.title: Agent Mode
agent-mode.desc: Autonomous multi-chapter authoring runs — mission control

chapter-revision-compare.title: Chapter Revision Compare
chapter-revision-compare.desc: Full editor diff for one chapter revision pair
chapter-revision-compare.empty: Open a chapter's revision diff from Agent Mode's review panel.

sceneRail.title: Scenes
sceneRail.empty: No scenes for this chapter (outline it in the composer).
sceneRail.notAnchored: Scene not anchored in the text yet — use ⚓ to link headings.
sceneRail.anchorNoop: {{count}} already anchored, {{unmatched}} without a matching heading.
sceneRail.anchorTitle: Link scenes to their headings in the text
sceneRail.addDisabled: Chapter has no outline yet (create it in the composer)

jsonEditor.empty: Open a resource as JSON from its panel ("Open as JSON").

planner.empty: Paste your novel-system markdown and Propose to start a plan run.

editor.empty: Select a chapter in the manuscript navigator to edit it here.

popout.openTitle: Open Compose in its own window

steering.heading: Steering rules
steering.subheading: Persistent author guidance injected into AI turns for this book.
steering.noBook: Open this panel from a book to author its steering rules.
steering.capReached: Rule limit reached ({{max}}). Delete a rule to add another.
steering.empty: No steering rules yet. Add one to guide the AI on this book.
steering.autoNote: Note: 'auto' currently behaves like 'manual' — it's included only when you reference it by #name. Automatic selection is coming later.

form.name: Name
form.bodyPlaceholder: Write the guidance the AI should follow…

error.duplicate: A rule with this name already exists in this book.
error.cap: That exceeds a limit (body ≤ 8000 chars, name ≤ 200, ≤ 20 rules) or an invalid mode.
error.other: Couldn't save the rule. Please try again.
error.forbidden: You do not have permission to edit steering for this book.

palette.quickOpenHint: Type to search chapters, scenes & arcs

desc.toggleSidebar: Show or hide the side bar
desc.startGuidedTour: A quick tour of the core panels

manuscript.title: Manuscript
manuscript.body: Chapters & scenes — the navigator that drives the dock. Built next.

bible.title: Story Bible
bible.body: Cast, world & canon entries. Coming soon.

search.title: Search
search.body: Full-text & semantic search across the book. Coming soon.

quality.title: Quality
quality.body: Critic scores, promise threads & canon issues. Coming soon.

bottomStub.jobs: Background jobs appear here once wired.
bottomStub.generation: Live generation progress appears here once wired.
bottomStub.issues: Quality issues appear here once wired.

intro.title: Welcome to Writing Studio
intro.subtitle: What are you here to do? This tailors your Welcome panel and quick tour — change it anytime from the Command Palette.

writer.title: Write a novel
writer.desc: Compose chapters with an AI co-writer, plan the system, edit the manuscript

worldbuilder.title: Build world & lore
worldbuilder.desc: Glossary entities, the wiki, and the knowledge graph

translator.title: Translate
translator.desc: Coverage matrix, bulk translate, per-chapter versions

enricher.title: Enrich existing text
enricher.desc: Find gaps, expand under-described entities, manage sources

manuscript.title: Your manuscript
manuscript.body: The Manuscript navigator drives the dock — pick a chapter or scene here to open it.

palette.title: Jump anywhere
palette.body: ⌘P jumps to a chapter/scene/arc; ⌘⇧P opens any Studio tool by name.

compose.title: Compose
compose.body: Your AI co-writer — draft, discuss, and apply prose changes here.

editor.title: Editor
editor.body: The manuscript editor — grammar, glossary hints, focus mode, and auto-save live here.

compose.title: Draft with your co-writer
compose.body: As a writer, this is where you'll spend a lot of time — ask for a first draft, a revision, or a sounding board for what happens next.

editor.title: Polish the prose
editor.body: Once a scene has words, refine it here — grammar checks, glossary hints, and focus mode all live in this editor.

planner.title: Plan the shape of the story
planner.body: Lay out arcs, chapters, and beats before or between writing sessions, so Compose and the Editor have a structure to write into.

glossary.title: Your world's dictionary
glossary.body: Every character, place, and item you've established lives here — search it, tag it, and keep it consistent.

wiki.title: Readable world articles
wiki.body: The wiki turns your glossary into narrative-style articles — a good way to review your world at a glance.

knowledge.title: The knowledge graph
knowledge.body: For deeper worldbuilding, the knowledge graph tracks how everything connects — entities, events, and relationships.

translation.title: Track translation coverage
translation.body: See which languages are covered, which chapters are stale, and kick off bulk translation runs from here.

enrichmentCompose.title: Fill in missing lore
enrichmentCompose.body: When a translation surfaces an under-described entity, use this panel to draft the missing lore before translating around it.

gaps.title: Find what's missing
gaps.body: This panel finds entities that are mentioned often but barely described, so you know exactly what to enrich next.

sources.title: Manage your grounding sources
sources.body: Enrichment drafts pull from the source material you manage here — keep it organized and license-tagged.

sharing.title: Control who sees this book
sharing.body: Set visibility, generate a share link, or invite collaborators — all from here.

bookSettings.title: Update the book's details
bookSettings.body: Title, cover, genre tags, and world link all live in Book Settings — update them any time.

userGuide.intro: Every tool Writing Studio can open, grouped the same way as the Command Palette.
